titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Bernstein Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 7 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 01:54

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 23-24 (printed email): Restricted: Not to be consulted before January 1, 2033.
Boxes 52 and 78-79 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 53, 106 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Computer files (email correspondence with Marjorie Perloff and Hank Lazer): Restricted until 2033. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Computer files (student recommendations): Restricted until 2033. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc., on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2013, 2021. Additional materials received under terms of original purchase, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Writings and Correspondence, 1980-2010. II. Notebooks, 1989-2010. III. Other Papers, 1950-2012. IV. Audiovisual Materials, circa 1970s-1980s. V. Electronic Media, 1990s-2010s. VII. May 2019 acquisition, 1969-1990. VIII. October 2021 acquisition, 1926-2020.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Bernstein (1950-) is an American poet, essayist, and scholar. He is a prominent figure among the Language poets and taught for many years at the University of Pennsylvania. Bernstein was awarded the 2019 Bollingen Prize for American Poetry.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Bernstein Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
The works in Series VI. Printed materials, 1970-2010 were transferred for separate bibliographic cataloging in 2021. Boxes 54-77 are no longer in the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Charles Bernstein papers, which span from 1950-2010s, contain extensive writings, along with other papers, programs, clippings, notebooks, audiovisual materials, and electronic media that document or are related to Bernstein's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Charles Bernstein Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain arrangement found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, correspondence, and notes by or related to Charles Bernstein.

did.unittitle: Typescripts, correspondence, drafts, notes and other materials

did.unittitle: Correspondence with manuscripts, drafts, and dissertations by other writers

Correspondence, manuscripts, news clippings, photographs, and ephemera

Proofs for Republics of Reality and writings of others

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains notebooks containing writings by or related to Charles Bernstein.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal papers, notes, clippings, posters, and ephemera notes related to Charles Bernstein's life and work.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, proofs, identification cards, photocopies of magazine articles and interviews

Materials from Shadowtime including contracts, proofs,notes, drafts, correspondence, printed material, news clippings and ephemera
Wednesdays at 4 plus posters and mailers

accessrestrict.p: Box 52 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audiovisual materials related to Charles Bernstein's life and work.

did.materialspec: Static and a high amount of hum can be heard on the tape. The program is distorted at times.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents on this audiotape reel are included below.

list.item: Praised Be the Lord to Home All Praise is [Due]
Called Upon Us and Given [They] Law
Yimcock and Hab Har Ba Torah

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight sub-series: Correspondence, 1965-2019; Writings, 1977-2018; Writings of others, 1987-2019; Teaching materials, 1972-2008; Reviews and press materials, 1982-2015; Personal papers, 1926-2016; Printed materials, 1964-2020; Computer files, circa 2006-2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The October 2021 acquisition consists of correspondence, writings, writings of others, teaching materials, reviews and press, personal papers, printed materials, digital photographs, and email documenting the work and life of Charles Bernstein. The materials document Bernstein's teaching career, work as a poet and scholar, and his role in publishing. In addition to documenting Bernstein's work, the papers document the work of many contemporary poets who corresponded or collaborated with Bernstein, or from whom Bernstein received submissions. The papers also include documentation related to Bernstein's parents and grandparents.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 106 (electronic media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of cards and notes clelebrating Bernstein's retirement, publishing solicitations, other greeting cards, and thank you notes from students. The subseries also includes a mailing list and family correspondence from the 1960s and 70s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to and from Bernstein's parents, as well as disciplinary letters relating to the 1969 Harvard Strike.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of printouts, proofs, lecture scripts and notes, and paste-ups for works by Bernstein, including collaborative works with Susan Bee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings of others consist of typescripts, manuscripts, offprints, photocopies, and other copies of work by other authors and shared with Bernstein.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 106 (electronic media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files contain a paperclip poem, Book object anthology, and Philly talks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teaching materials consist of evaluations of courses taught by Bernstein and course reading packets.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reviews and press materials consist of clippings, photocopies, printouts, press releases, proofs, typescripts, and other materials documenting Bernstein's appearances in academic literature, popular media, local news, and elsewhere.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers include correspondence and other papers related to Bernstein's parents, papers relating to Bernstein's grandparents Edward B. Kegel and Bertha Kegel, passports, a driver's license, term papers, Bernstein's diploma, and other materils from Harvard, artwork signed to Bernstein from a friend, and Bernstein's rolodex.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials include conference materials, a calendar, Harvard alumni materials, offprints, pamphlets, posters, and press catalogs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 106 (electronic media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Harvard and Radcliffe class of 1972 forty fifth anniversary report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Computer files consist of digital photographs and email.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer files only, no physical media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital photographs document Charles Bernstein's colleagues and friends at readings, events, parties, and elsewhere. Most photographs are by Bernstein. Subjects include many contemporaries of Bernstein, including Susan Bee, Gerrit Lansing, Claudia Rankine, Marjorie Perloff, Rachel Blau Duplessis, Susan Howe, Trevor Winkfield, Tony Towle, and many others. Also includes photographs of trips to Korea, China, Portugal, Stockholm, and São Paulo.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Email correspondence with Marjorie Perloff and Hank Lazer is restricted until 2033.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Emails document collaborations wtih Amy Sillman, Ted Greenwald, Susan Howe, Marjorie Perloff, Feng Yi, Ian Probstein, Regis Bonvicino, and others. Emails also document Bernstein's work on the University of Alabama Press poetics series, the operation of PennSound and EPC (Electronic Poetry Center), poetry submissions, rejections, and other publishing matters, and extensive correspondence with many poets and scholars, including Chinese scholars.
